body:
### Describe the bug

The proper way to advance iterators is to use increment() from cython.operator, but the following code should probably give a sensible compiler error instead of an outright compiler crash.

### Code to reproduce the behaviour:

```cython
# distutils: language=c++

from libcpp.vector cimport vector

cdef f():
    cdef vector[int] v

    it = v.begin()
    while it != v.end():
        it += 1
```

```
cythonbug.pyx:5:0: Compiler crash in AnalyseExpressionsTransform

ModuleNode.body = StatListNode(cythonbug.pyx:3:0)
StatListNode.stats[1] = CFuncDefNode(cythonbug.pyx:5:0,
    args = [...]/0,
    c_compile_guard = '',
    modifiers = [...]/0,
    outer_attrs = [...]/2,
    visibility = 'private')

Compiler crash traceback from this point on:
  File "Cython/Compiler/Visitor.py", line 174, in Cython.Compiler.Visitor.TreeVisitor._visit
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/ParseTreeTransforms.py", line 2867, in visit_FuncDefNode
    node.local_scope.infer_types()
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/Symtab.py", line 1222, in infer_types
    get_type_inferer().infer_types(self)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/TypeInference.py", line 477, in infer_types
    if not resolve_assignments(assignments):
           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/TypeInference.py", line 442, in resolve_assignments
    infer_name_node_type(node)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/TypeInference.py", line 405, in infer_name_node_type
    node_type = spanning_type(
        types, entry.might_overflow, scope)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/TypeInference.py", line 542, in safe_spanning_type
    result_type = simply_type(reduce(find_spanning_type, types))
                              ~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/TypeInference.py", line 524, in find_spanning_type
    result_type = PyrexTypes.spanning_type(type1, type2)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/PyrexTypes.py", line 5485, in spanning_type
    span_type = _spanning_type(type1, type2)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.13/dist-packages/Cython/Compiler/PyrexTypes.py", line 5492, in _spanning_type
    if type1.is_numeric and type2.is_numeric:
       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'is_numeric'
```

### Expected behaviour

Something like "Numeric operation invalid for this type."

### OS

Linux

### Python version

3.13.3

### Cython version

3.4.2

### Additional context

_No response_

comment_data.body:
It definitely shouldn't crash.

We do support overloaded `operator+` on c++ classes and vector iterators should be random access so that should probably work anyway.

title: [BUG] compiler crash when abusing iterators
